description: Lin · Head of Product · Bi-weekly — May-24 thread-follow regression (6ae9ead8-32f)

prep.name: Lin
prep.notes: Lin has delivered two major features ahead of schedule but has worked late for about three weeks. I need to understand whether this pace was a temporary push or is becoming a default. Because Lin tends to brush off concern, I should ask directly what is driving the load now that those launches are done. I also want clarity on what work only Lin should own versus what could be delegated, since sustained late nights at this level can hide a distribution or autonomy problem.

focusPoints.label: Three weeks of late nights — pace and what's driving it.
focusPoints.reason: Notes flag consistent late nights alongside two ahead-of-schedule deliveries. Could be a personal push through a meaningful stretch, an unsustainable baseline forming, or something outside work bleeding in. Lin brushes off concern, which makes it worth naming plainly rather than asking indirectly.

focusPoints.label: What's actually in flight now that those two features shipped.
focusPoints.reason: Two major features are done. Worth understanding what Lin is moving toward next and whether the pace is being carried forward by default or by a deliberate choice about what comes next.

focusPoints.label: How much of the load is theirs alone versus the team's.
focusPoints.reason: Whether the late nights reflect a distribution problem — work that could sit with others but isn't — or genuine senior-level depth work only Lin can do. At Head of Product, the two look similar from the outside but have very different implications.

answers: The last stretch shipped, but it was heavier than I made it sound.
Late nights came from carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews.
Some of that work could have moved to the team sooner.
I am proud of the outcomes, but the pace should not be normal.
I still do not get enough substantive feedback on key decisions.
I want clearer ownership boundaries so I am not default escalation.
Next I want to hand off one stream end-to-end and stay out of the middle.
If we reset scope now, the next month is sustainable.

may24Bank.name: Before we get into specifics, how do you think the last few weeks have actually gone from your side?
I've noticed you've been working late most nights for a few weeks — what's actually driving that?
If the next three months looked like the last three weeks, what would break first?
Of the work that filled those late nights, how much of it could only you have done?
Now that both features are out, what are you actually moving toward next — and is that a deliberate choice or just what's next in the queue?
Where is the team's capacity right now compared to yours?
Did the recognition for those two deliveries land properly for you, or did anything feel missing?
What's currently on your plate that you're not sure should still be yours?
What's the one thing that, if it changed before we next meet, would make the next two weeks noticeably easier?

brief.coreIssue: Lin has specific performance problems with late nights that need to be addressed directly.
brief.openingQuestion: What specific issues are causing you to work late every night this week?
listenFor: whether Lin acknowledges the late-night problem
if Lin takes ownership of the workload
brief.goodOutcome: Lin agrees to stop working late by next week.
brief.suggestedAction: Schedule a check-in next month to confirm Lin is leaving on time.
